paraphrase.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda
paraphrase.body: Qatar Airways has announced plans to resume flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, starting 15 July, according to a statement from the airline. The carrier will operate four weekly flights to Helsinki, increasing to daily services from 1 August. Tokyo Haneda will also receive four weekly flights from 15 July. HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani welcomed the expansion, which brings the airline's network to over 160 destinations. The move underscores Qatar Airways' commitment to enhancing connectivity and supporting travel demand, the airline said.
key_facts: Resumption of flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from 15 July
Four weekly flights to Helsinki, increasing to daily from 1 August
Four weekly flights to Tokyo Haneda from 15 July

factual_grounding.note: GROUNDING verification, NOT fact-checking. This confirms the draft's specifics (names, numbers, dates) trace back to the source brief. It does NOT verify the brief itself is true — if the brief is wrong, the draft can be perfectly grounded and still factually incorrect. Ungrounded specifics are items a reviewer must check before publish.
